docs.title: Home
docs.text:
fr24 is a library for downloading data from Flightradar24 using gRPC and JSON.

It supports querying live feed snapshots/playback, trajectory history and other miscellaneous metadata.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
PipPoetry

Create a new virtual environment and run:

pip install git+https://github.com/cathaypacific8747/fr24.git

poetry add git+https://github.com/cathaypacific8747/fr24.git

For a development version, clone the repository and install it:

PipPoetry 
git clone https://github.com/cathaypacific8747/fr24.git
cd fr24
pip install .

git clone https://github.com/cathaypacific8747/fr24.git
cd fr24
poetry install
poetry shell

docs.title: Core
docs.text:
Most of the core code is developed in a functional style, though the fr24.core.FR24 class provides a wrapper around them. Every response is automatically saved into the following directories:

  • Feed
    • feed/playback/{timestamp_ms}.parquet
    • feed/live/{timestmap_ms}.parquet
  • Flight history
    • flight_list/reg/{reg}.parquet, or
    • flight_list/flight/{iata_flight_num}.parquet
  • Playback
    • playback/metadata/{fr24_id}.parquet
    • playback/track/{fr24_id}.parquet
    • playback/track_ems/{fr24_id}.parquet

Note that the fr24 flight ID is in base 10, not hex.

The current implementation is poor and will be reworked in the future.

docs.title: livefeed_message_create
docs.text:
livefeed_message_create(
    north: float = 50,
    south: float = 40,
    west: float = 0,
    east: float = 10,
    stats: bool = False,
    limit: int = 1500,
    maxage: int = 14400,
    fields: list[str] = ["flight", "reg", "route", "type"],
    **kwargs: Any
) -> LiveFeedRequest

Create the LiveFeedRequest protobuf message

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default north float

North latitude

50 south float

South latitude

40 west float

West longitude

0 east float

East longitude

10 stats bool

Include stats of the given area

False limit int

Max number of flights

1500 maxage int

Max age since last update, seconds

14400 fields list[str]

fields to include - for unauthenticated users, max 4 fields. When authenticated, squawk, vspeed, airspace, logo_id and age can be included

['flight', 'reg', 'route', 'type']

docs.title: flight_list async
docs.text:
flight_list(
    client: AsyncClient,
    reg: None | str = None,
    flight: None | str = None,
    page: int = 1,
    limit: int = 10,
    timestamp: (
        int | datetime | Timestamp | str | None
    ) = "now",
    auth: None | Authentication = None,
) -> FlightList

Fetch metadata/history of flights for a given aircraft or flight number.

Includes basic information such as status, O/D, scheduled/estimated/real times: see fr24.types.fr24.FlightData for more details.

Use either reg or flight to query. To determine if there are more pages to query, check the response .result.response.page.more.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default client AsyncClient

HTTPX async client

required reg None | str

Aircraft registration (e.g. B-HUJ)

None flight None | str

Flight number (e.g. CX8747)

None page int

Page number

1 limit int

Number of results per page (max 100)

10 timestamp int | datetime | Timestamp | str | None

Show flights with ATD before this Unix timestamp

'now' auth None | Authentication

Authentication data

None

docs.title: airport_list async
docs.text:
airport_list(
    client: AsyncClient,
    airport: str,
    mode: (
        Literal["arrivals"]
        | Literal["departures"]
        | Literal["ground"]

    page: int = 1,
    limit: int = 10,
    timestamp: (
        int | datetime | Timestamp | str | None
    ) = "now",
    auth: None | Authentication = None,
) -> AirportList

Fetch aircraft arriving, departing or on ground at a given airport.

Returns on ground/scheduled/estimated/real times: see fr24.types.fr24.FlightListItem for more details.

Parameters:

HTTPX async client

required airport str

IATA airport code (e.g. HKG)

required mode Literal['arrivals'] | Literal['departures'] | Literal['ground']

arrivals, departures or on ground aircraft

required page int

Page number

1 limit int

Number of results per page (max 100)

10 timestamp int | datetime | Timestamp | str | None

Show flights with STA before this Unix timestamp

'now' auth None | Authentication

Authentication data

None

docs.title: login async
docs.text:
login(client: AsyncClient) -> None | Authentication

Create credentials for the user (if any). If the environment variables:

  • subscription_key and token is set: it returns the pair
  • username and password is set: it fetches the token and subscription key from the API
  • otherwise, it returns None

docs.title: FR24
docs.text:
FR24(cache_dir: str = user_cache_dir('fr24'))

Populate http clients and build the cache directory tree.

Check the cache directory for more details.

docs.title: cache_flight_list_upsert async
docs.text:
cache_flight_list_upsert(
    reg: None | str = None,
    flight: None | str = None,
    page: int = 1,
    limit: int = 100,
    timestamp: (
        int | datetime | Timestamp | str | None
    ) = "now",
    overwrite: bool = False,
) -> Path

Iteratively update the flight list cache by querying in batches, whenever a duplicate is found in that batch, stop querying.

The "Estimated XX:XX" status will also be updated to the latest.

docs.title: flight_list_schema module-attribute
docs.text:
flight_list_schema = schema(
    [
        field("flight_id", uint64()),
        field("number", string()),
        field("callsign", string()),
        field("icao24", uint32()),
        field("registration", string()),
        field("typecode", string()),
        field("origin", string()),
        field("destination", string()),
        field("status", string()),
        field("STOD", timestamp("s")),
        field("ETOD", timestamp("s")),
        field("ATOD", timestamp("s")),
        field("STOA", timestamp("s")),
        field("ETOA", timestamp("s")),
        field("ATOA", timestamp("s")),
    ]
)

docs.title: playback_track_schema module-attribute
docs.text:
playback_track_schema = schema(
    [
        field("timestamp", timestamp("s")),
        field("latitude", float32()),
        field("longitude", float32()),
        field("altitude", int32()),
        field("ground_speed", int16()),
        field("vertical_speed", int16()),
        field("heading", int16()),
        field("squawk", uint16()),
    ]
)

        field("timestamp", timestamp("s")),
        field("ias", int16()),
        field("mach", int16()),
        field("mcp", int32()),
        field("fms", int32()),
        field("autopilot", int8()),
        field("oat", int8()),
        field("track", float32()),
        field("roll", float32()),
        field("qnh", uint16()),
        field("wind_dir", int16()),
        field("wind_speed", int16()),
        field("precision", uint8()),
        field("altitude_gps", int32()),
        field("emergency", uint8()),
        field("tcas_acas", uint8()),
        field("heading", uint16()),
    ]
)

    "AirportRequest",

        "code": str,
        "device": Optional[str],
        "fleet": Optional[str],

        "limit": int,

        "plugin-setting[schedule][mode]": str,
        "plugin-setting[schedule][timestamp]": int,
        "token": Optional[str],
    },
    total=False,
)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
• List all commands and show help:

    fr24 --help

  • Dumping the current state vectors in a tidy parquet file:

    fr24 feed live
fr24 feed playback --timestamp 1702839380
fr24 feed playback --time 2023-12-17T18:56:00

  • TUI

    fr24 tui

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
You may want to be authenticated to access more history: set the environment variables fr24_username and fr24_password, then check that you are properly authenticated:

fr24 auth show

You may also create a configuration file in the config directory: an example of it can be found at fr24.example.conf.

docs.title: Directories
docs.text:
Check the location of the config and cache directories with: 

fr24 dirs
Here are possible outputs:

OS Config File Cache Directory Linux $HOME/.config/fr24/fr24.conf $HOME/.cache/fr24 macOS $HOME/Library/Preferences/fr24.conf $HOME/Library/Caches/fr24 Windows %LOCALAPPDATA%\Acme\fr24\Cache\fr24.conf %LOCALAPPDATA%\Acme\fr24\Cache

docs.title: Library
docs.text:
Most code is developed in asynchronous mode: if you are not familiar with that programming style, wrap your code in an async function called async_main, and

  • run asyncio.run(main()) in a Python script;
  • run await main() in a Jupyter notebook.

You can find usage examples in under scripts/.

Note

Pyarrow do not come with stubs: generate them with 

stubgen -p pyarrow -o $PATH_TO_SITE_PACKAGES
